_comment: Example config for qwen-coprocessor-stack. Copy to ~/.qwen-coprocessor-stack/config.json and edit. Unknown top-level fields (this _comment included) are silently ignored by the supervisor.
_optional_fields: default_extensions: array of extension names applied to every spawn (e.g. ["serena", "context7"]); omit to use CLI defaults. /qwen-stack:defaults manages this.

session_budget: { max_context_tokens, max_tool_calls } overriding the per-backend default. Omit to let the supervisor derive max_context_tokens from each chosen backend's ctx_size (floor(0.85 * ctx_size); RDR-002 v0.7). 0 at any tier means 'no cap' and is preserved as-is. /qwen-stack:budget manages this.

backend.modality: optional 'text' | 'multimodal' | 'embedding' | 'rerank'. Unset = 'text'. Chat router only picks text/multimodal; qwen_oneshot_vision requires multimodal; qwen_embed/qwen_rerank require their matching modality.

backend.api_key / api_key_env: optional bearer-token credential for remote OpenAI-compat endpoints (OpenRouter, Together, Fireworks, ...). The supervisor sends 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' on every request. Prefer api_key_env (read from process env at request time, so rotations apply on next request) over api_key (literal in the config file invites accidental commits). Auth values are never logged.

backend.headers: optional Record<string,string> of additional request headers, merged after the supervisor's built-ins (Content-Type, Authorization). Useful for provider-required attribution (OpenRouter's HTTP-Referer / X-Title) or custom routing tags.

backends._comment: Optional embedding backend (e.g. llama-server -m bge-m3.gguf --embeddings). Drop the block if you don't run one.
Optional rerank backend (e.g. llama-server -m bge-reranker-v2-m3.gguf --reranking). Drop the block if you don't run one.
Example remote bearer-gated backend (OpenRouter / Together / Fireworks shape). Drop the block if you don't use one — shown here only to document the api_key_env + headers fields.
